tasks.task: Update getFileMetadata() to exclude soft-deleted files
Update listFiles() to exclude soft-deleted files (Story 4.5)

ac.title: DELETE /files/:id endpoint created
ac.requirements: - Route: DELETE /api/files/:id - Guards: JwtAuthGuard, PermissionsGuard - Permission: FILES.DELETE (or file owner) - Path parameter: id (UUID validation) - Returns success message on deletion

ac.title: Soft delete implemented
ac.requirements: - Sets deletedAt to current timestamp - Does NOT delete metadata from database - Does NOT delete file from S3 (deferred to cleanup job) - File excluded from all queries (WHERE deletedAt IS NULL)

ac.title: Deletion access validated
ac.requirements: - Checks: file exists AND file.domainID === currentUserDomainID - Checks: file.userID === currentUserID OR user has FILES.DELETE permission - Returns 403 Forbidden if user not authorized - Returns 404 Not Found if file doesn't exist or already deleted

ac.title: Deletion response validated
ac.requirements: - Returns 200 OK on success - Response: { success: true, message: "File deleted successfully" } - Subsequent GET /files/:id returns 404 Not Found - Subsequent GET /files/:id/download returns 404 Not Found

ac.title: Deletion logged
ac.requirements: - Logs file deletion event (info level) - Includes: fileID, s3Key, userID, domainID, timestamp - Logs who deleted the file (currentUserID)

ac.title: Soft-deleted files excluded from list endpoint
ac.requirements: - GET /files excludes files with deletedAt !== null - Admin with FILES.VIEW_ALL also doesn't see soft-deleted files - No way to list deleted files via API (future restore feature)

doc.title: Epic 4 Technical Specification - File Management (AWS S3)
doc.snippet: Complete acceptance criteria for Story 4.4 (AC-4.4.1 through AC-4.4.6). Soft delete pattern: Sets deletedAt timestamp, preserves DB metadata, defers S3 cleanup to scheduled job. Access control: Owner or FILES.DELETE permission required, domain isolation enforced.

doc.title: Boilerplate - Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: File deletion with soft delete pattern. DELETE /files/:id endpoint with permission check (FILES.DELETE or owner). Soft-delete metadata (deletedAt set), S3 cleanup deferred to cleanup job (future Epic 11). Domain isolation mandatory.

doc.title: Story 4.3: File Download (Pre-Signed URL)
doc.snippet: FilesService.getFileMetadata() method implements comprehensive access control (domain isolation + ownership check + permission-based access). Already checks file.deletedAt === null (soft-deleted files excluded). Reuse this method in deleteFile() to avoid duplicating validation logic.

doc.title: Story 4.2: File Upload Endpoint (Single & Multiple)
doc.snippet: File entity created in Prisma schema with deletedAt field (nullable DateTime). Indexes on deletedAt for query performance. FILES.DELETE permission constant defined in permissions.constant.ts and synced to database.

artifact.reason: REUSE THIS METHOD in deleteFile() for access validation. Already implements: file exists check, domain isolation (file.domainID === currentUserDomainID), ownership check (file.userID === currentUserID), permission check (hasViewPermission), soft-delete filtering (deletedAt === null). Returns NotFoundException for not found/wrong domain/soft-deleted, ForbiddenException for unauthorized.

Add new DELETE /files/:id endpoint. Follow existing pattern: @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, PermissionsGuard), @Permission('FILES', ActionEnum.DELETE), @Param('id', ParseUUIDPipe), @CurrentUser() user: JwtPayload. Extract domainID and userID from user, call FilesService.deleteFile().
FILES.DELETE permission constant already defined (Story 4.2). Use for permission decorator: @Permission('FILES', ActionEnum.DELETE). Permission already synced to database via permission-sync script.

Add unit tests for deleteFile() method: owner deletes (success), user with FILES.DELETE deletes (success), file not found (NotFoundException), wrong domain (NotFoundException), already deleted (NotFoundException), not owner + no permission (ForbiddenException). Test soft-delete filtering in getFileMetadata().

Add E2E tests for DELETE /files/:id: owner can delete (200 OK), invalid UUID (400), non-existent file (404), wrong domain (404), already deleted (404), unauthorized (401), user without permission (403). Test end-to-end: Upload → Delete → GET returns 404, Upload → Delete → Download returns 404.

File entity has deletedAt field (DateTime?, nullable) for soft delete. Indexes on deletedAt for performance. Soft delete implementation: UPDATE File SET deletedAt = NOW() WHERE id = fileId. All queries filter: WHERE deletedAt IS NULL.

node.package: Core NestJS framework - Injectable, exceptions, decorators
Express adapter for NestJS, Multer integration
Rate limiting (used in upload endpoint, not deletion)
Prisma ORM client for database operations
AWS S3 SDK (S3Service - not used in Story 4.4, cleanup deferred)

devDependencies.package: HTTP assertion library for E2E tests
Prisma CLI for migrations and schema management

constraints.constraint: REUSE FilesService.getFileMetadata() for access validation - DO NOT duplicate validation logic
Soft delete only: Set deletedAt timestamp, DO NOT delete database record, DO NOT call S3Service.deleteFile()
S3 cleanup deferred to scheduled job (Epic 11) - cleanup job will query deletedAt > 7 days → delete from S3 → hard delete DB record
All queries must filter: WHERE deletedAt IS NULL (getFileMetadata, listFiles, generateDownloadUrl)
Error handling: 404 for not found/wrong domain/already deleted (consistent response, prevent info leakage), 403 for unauthorized
Domain isolation mandatory: file.domainID === currentUserDomainID on all operations
Access control: Owner (file.userID === currentUserID) OR user has FILES.DELETE permission
Logging required: Log deletion event (info level) with fileID, s3Key, userID, domainID, currentUserID (who deleted)
TypeScript strict mode: All code must pass strict type checking
Controller-Service pattern: FilesController → FilesService.deleteFile() → Prisma update
Guards pattern: @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, PermissionsGuard) on DELETE endpoint
Response format: { success: true, message: "File deleted successfully" }

interface.name: FilesService.deleteFile()
interface.signature: async deleteFile( fileId: string, domainID: string, userID: string, hasDeletePermission: boolean ): Promise<void>
interface.description: Soft delete file by setting deletedAt timestamp. Validates access using getFileMetadata() (reuse existing validation). Logs deletion event. Does NOT delete from S3.

interface.name: FilesController.deleteFile()
interface.description: DELETE /api/files/:id endpoint. Validates UUID, extracts user from JWT, calls FilesService.deleteFile(), returns success response.

interface.name: FilesService.getFileMetadata()
interface.signature: async getFileMetadata( fileId: string, domainID: string, userID: string, hasViewPermission: boolean ): Promise<File>
interface.description: EXISTING METHOD from Story 4.3. Validates file access: file exists, domain match, deletedAt === null, owner or has permission. REUSE in deleteFile() for validation (call getFileMetadata before soft delete). Throws NotFoundException or ForbiddenException.

interface.name: Prisma File Entity
interface.signature: model File { id String @id @default(uuid()) domainID String userID String filename String originalName String mimeType String size Int s3Key String s3Bucket String createdAt DateTime @default(now()) deletedAt DateTime? // Soft delete field user User @relation(fields: [userID], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade) @@index([domainID]) @@index([userID]) @@index([mimeType]) @@index([deletedAt]) // Performance for soft-delete queries @@index([s3Key]) }
interface.description: File entity with deletedAt field for soft delete. Update operation: prisma.file.update({ where: { id }, data: { deletedAt: new Date() } }). All queries filter: where: { deletedAt: null }.

tests.standards: Testing follows NestJS testing patterns with Jest. Unit tests use mocking for PrismaService and S3Service. E2E tests use supertest with MinIO for S3 operations. Test organization: src/**/*.spec.ts for unit tests, test/**/*.e2e-spec.ts for E2E tests. Coverage requirements: All service methods must have unit tests, all endpoints must have E2E tests. Test pattern: Arrange (setup), Act (execute), Assert (verify). Use describe blocks for grouping, beforeEach for setup, expect for assertions.

ideas.idea: Unit test: FilesService.deleteFile() with owner - should set deletedAt timestamp and return void
Unit test: FilesService.deleteFile() with user having FILES.DELETE permission - should succeed
Unit test: FilesService.deleteFile() with file not found - should throw NotFoundException
Unit test: FilesService.deleteFile() with wrong domain - should throw NotFoundException (prevent info leakage)
Unit test: FilesService.deleteFile() with already deleted file (deletedAt !== null) - should throw NotFoundException
Unit test: FilesService.deleteFile() with not owner and no FILES.DELETE permission - should throw ForbiddenException
Unit test: FilesService.getFileMetadata() with soft-deleted file - should throw NotFoundException (verify deletedAt filter works)
E2E test: DELETE /files/:id as owner - should return 200 OK with success message
E2E test: DELETE /files/:id with invalid UUID - should return 400 Bad Request
E2E test: DELETE /files/:id with non-existent file - should return 404 Not Found
E2E test: DELETE /files/:id with wrong domain - should return 404 Not Found
E2E test: DELETE /files/:id with already deleted file - should return 404 Not Found
E2E test: DELETE /files/:id without JWT - should return 401 Unauthorized
E2E test: DELETE /files/:id as user without FILES.DELETE permission and not owner - should return 403 Forbidden
E2E test: Upload file → Delete → GET /files/:id - should return 404 Not Found (verify soft-delete excludes from queries)
E2E test: Upload file → Delete → GET /files/:id/download - should return 404 Not Found (verify download also excludes soft-deleted)
E2E test: Upload file → Delete → Verify file still in S3 (soft delete, S3 not affected) - should find file in MinIO bucket
